briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Approve Housing Agreement for 388-Unit Rental Development at 6616 Telford Avenue
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw (Bylaw No. 14790) that would authorize a formal housing agreement with the developer of a new rental building at 6616 Telford Avenue in Burnaby. If approved, the agreement would lock in rules for 304 below-market rental units and 84 market-rate rental units — setting rent limits, tenant eligibility requirements, and long-term affordability conditions that would run with the property permanently.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council adoption vote — this agreement will permanently affect rental rules at 6616 Telford Avenue.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Housing Agreement Bylaw 14790 (6616 Telford Ave) — third reading passed, awaiting adoption

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Bosa Properties housing agreement for 94 non-market rentals at 5980 Kathleen Ave
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would authorize a formal housing agreement with Bosa Properties (Metrotown) Inc. for a 35-storey rental building at 5980 Kathleen Ave. If approved, the agreement would lock in 94 below-market rental units — 92 reserved for tenants displaced from prior sites, and 2 for income-qualifying households — with rents and tenant protections governed by the City's Tenant Assistance Policy.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — this affects affordable rental supply and tenant rights in Metrotown.
briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Construction & Development, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on final approval of purpose-built rental townhouse rezoning in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on the final adoption of a rezoning bylaw (Bylaw 14590) that would officially change the land-use rules for several properties on Bevan Street and Marine Drive in the Edmonds area. The rezoning, proposed by Mosaic Avenue Developments, would allow a purpose-built rental townhouse project called 'Bevan Townhomes' to move forward on what is a City-owned, non-market housing site.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision on January 27, 2026 — this rezoning affects nearby properties and rental housing supply.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Bylaw 14590 rezoning final adoption vote — Council meeting

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Tenant

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Rezoning approval for Bevan Townhomes development in Burnaby
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on final adoption of a bylaw that would rezone a parcel of land to allow the 'Bevan Townhomes' project, a townhouse development designed by Ekistics Architecture. The bylaw has already passed three readings and a public hearing — Council must now give final approval (called 'adoption') to make the rezoning official.
briefs.action_required: Check if your property or business is near this rezoning area — construction activity may affect you.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Zoning Bylaw Amendment No. 23, 2023 — final adoption vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on Burnaby's proposed 2026–2030 Five-Year Financial Plan
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would adopt the city's five-year financial plan covering 2026 to 2030. If approved, the plan would set out how the city expects to raise and spend money each year — starting with roughly $873 million in revenue and $1.35 billion in total spending in 2026, funded mainly through property taxes (44.8%) and fees for city services.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council vote — property tax rates and city fees in this plan could affect your business costs.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning of 1030 Sperling Ave, Burnaby to Allow 'Sperling Court' Development
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed bylaw that would rezone (change the permitted land use) for the property at 1030 Sperling Ave in Burnaby. If approved, the land would be redeveloped under a plan called 'Sperling Court,' designed by Kennith Kim Architecture Inc., and all construction on the site would have to follow that approved plan.
briefs.action_required: Monitor the Burnaby Council meeting agenda for the vote date if this rezoning affects your property or business.

briefs.title: Council to consider 2025 open resolutions and bylaw tracking status update
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to receive a status update on all 2025 council resolutions and bylaws — tracking which items are completed, in progress, or outstanding. The report also covers unfinished 2024 items. Starting in 2026, these updates will shift from quarterly to twice-yearly reporting to reduce administrative workload while keeping Council and the public informed.
briefs.action_required: Review the tracking index for any bylaws or rezoning decisions that may affect your property or business operations.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Consider Approving Burnaby's 2026 Child Care Resource Group Workplan
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on endorsing the 2026 workplan for the Child Care Resources Group (CCRG) — an advisory body that guides the City on child care matters. Council will also consider approving $3,000 in funding to support a Child Care Provider Appreciation event in May 2026 and related professional development activities.
briefs.action_required: If you run a child care facility in Burnaby, watch for the council decision — it may affect approval processes for new or modified spaces.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Child Care Provider Appreciation Event — CCRG
Burnaby Child Care Action Plan update — scheduled start

briefs.title: Council to vote on adding anti-Palestinian racism to Burnaby's Anti-Racism Framework
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on whether to officially recognize anti-Palestinian racism (APR) as a distinct form of racism and add its definition to the existing Burnaby Anti-Racism Framework. The definition, developed by the Arab Canadian Lawyers Association, describes APR as a form of anti-Arab racism that silences, excludes, or dehumanizes Palestinians or their narratives. If approved, this would be the first time the City formally names and includes APR in its anti-racism policy.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it may affect how your workplace anti-racism policies are expected to align with City standards.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $240,000 contract renewal for Burnaby recyclable materials hauling
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a contract with GFL Environmental Inc. to haul recyclable materials from City facilities to an approved recycling depot. The proposed one-year renewal would run from February 1, 2026 to January 31, 2027, at an estimated cost of $240,000 including taxes. This would be the third and final renewal under the original 2022 contract.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on January 27, 2026 — this affects who hauls Burnaby's recyclables.

key_dates.label: GFL Environmental contract renewal start date — Burnaby recyclable materials hauling
GFL Environmental contract renewal end date — Burnaby recyclable materials hauling

briefs.title: Council to Vote on $123,475 Contract Change for Burnaby Sewer Monitoring Sensors
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving a contract change worth $123,475 (including taxes) with ADS Environmental Technologies Inc. This proposed change would cover 45 replacement battery packs, 20 high-gain antennas, and 12 months of wireless monitoring subscriptions for the city's 45 sewer water level sensors — devices that detect blockages and help prevent sewer overflows across Burnaby.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on January 27, 2026 — this affects city sewer infrastructure contracts.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — sewer sensor contract change approval
Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan Council approval — Sewer Utility Budget
Contract Renewal #1 expiry — ADS Environmental sewer sensor contract

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.04M contract for Burnaby Village Museum meadow and plaza upgrades
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a construction contract worth approximately $1,039,028 (including taxes) to Holaco Construction (1997) Ltd. The work would upgrade the meadow and Tram Barn Plaza at Burnaby Village Museum — adding a paved plaza surface, power and water hookups for food trucks and events, better drainage, lighting, and improved access between the meadow and the carousel building.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on January 27 — food truck operators should note new power/water hookups may open event opportunities.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Burnaby Village Museum contract award decision
Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan approval — project funding confirmed

briefs.title: Council to vote on $3.8M security system upgrade for Burnaby city facilities
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $3.8 million contract to Paladin Technologies Inc. to upgrade and unify security systems across city facilities. The five-year deal (February 2026 – January 2031) would cover new cameras, access control systems, and ongoing maintenance — consolidating separate security setups into one city-wide platform.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on January 27 — this contract affects security technology suppliers and installers.

key_dates.label: Burnaby facility security contract start date — Paladin Technologies agreement takes effect
Burnaby facility security contract end date — five-year agreement expires

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.3M traffic control contract with The Universal Group
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth up to $1,300,000 (including taxes) to The Universal Group for flagging and traffic control services. If approved, the contract would run from February 1, 2026 to January 31, 2027, with up to four one-year renewal options.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the January 27, 2026 council decision if you compete for City traffic control contracts.

key_dates.label: Burnaby flagging & traffic control contract start date — if approved

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on $1.3M contract for flagging and traffic control services
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth up to $1,300,000 (including taxes) to Metro Traffic Ltd. for flagging and traffic control services. If approved, the contract would run from February 1, 2026 to January 31, 2027, with up to four one-year renewal options, covering city construction, maintenance, and emergency work on an as-needed basis.
briefs.action_required: If you provide traffic control services, watch for future RFSOs — this contract may be renewed annually for up to 4 more years.

key_dates.label: Metro Traffic Ltd. flagging contract proposed start date
Metro Traffic Ltd. flagging contract initial term end date

briefs.title: Council to vote on $813,540 contract to replace aging air handling unit at Eileen Dailly Pool
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract worth $813,540 (including taxes) to Fortius Mechanical Inc. to replace a worn-out air handling unit — the system that moves and filters air — at the Eileen Dailly Leisure Pool & Fitness Centre. The unit has reached the end of its useful life and must be replaced to keep the lobby and fitness areas properly ventilated.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on January 27, 2026 — this is a public facility maintenance contract.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Eileen Dailly Pool AHU1 contract award
Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan approval — funding this project

briefs.title: Council to vote on $2.625M contract for Burnaby outdoor pool rehabilitation
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $2,625,000 contract to ETRO Construction Limited to repair and restore aging outdoor pool tanks across the city. The work — starting with Central Park Outdoor Pool as the top priority for 2026–2027 — would include sandblasting, concrete repairs, waterproofing, and pipe relining to keep the pools safe and operational.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — construction activity near Central Park Outdoor Pool may affect nearby businesses.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan — Council approval expected

briefs.title: Council to vote on $5.75M contract for Burnaby fire station infrastructure upgrades
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a $5.75 million contract (including taxes) to Holaco Construction (1997) Ltd. to upgrade emergency infrastructure at two Burnaby fire stations. The work would replace aging underground fuel tanks with above-ground fueling systems and install new emergency generators — keeping both stations fully operational during the upgrades.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if you are a construction contractor — this contract has already been awarded pending approval.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — Emergency Facilities Infrastructure Upgrades contract award
Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan approval — funding for this project

briefs.title: Council to vote on cost-sharing agreement with School District 41 for 2026 election
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on allowing the City's Chief Election Officer to sign a cost-sharing agreement with Burnaby School District 41. Under this proposed deal, the City would run the School Trustee election on the School District's behalf — as it has done in past elections — with the School District contributing school buildings as voting locations and staff time to help offset costs.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision if your business is near a school used as a voting location on October 17, 2026.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026 General Local Election day

briefs.title: Council to Consider Sending Feedback on BC's Speculation and Vacancy Tax Program
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on submitting a formal response to the BC Ministry of Finance as part of the province's annual consultation on the Speculation and Vacancy Tax (SVT) — a tax on homes left empty or used for speculation. The proposed response would ask the province to share SVT revenue directly with municipalities like Burnaby, and to give Burnaby the power to run its own Empty Homes Tax, similar to Vancouver's.
briefs.action_required: If you own vacant or investment property in Burnaby, watch for the SVT rate increases taking effect January 2026.

key_dates.label: BC Speculation and Vacancy Tax rate increases take effect

briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance, Construction & Development

briefs.title: Council to vote on $195M loan guarantee bylaws for two Burnaby affordable housing projects
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on two proposed bylaws that would allow the City to back — that is, guarantee repayment of — loans totalling $195M for the Burnaby Housing Authority Corporation (BHA). The BHA is applying for a $100M loan for a housing project at 10th & Newcombe and a $95M loan for a project in Edmonds at Marine & Bevan, together delivering 386 non-market housing units. City staff do not expect the City will ever need to actually borrow the money, but the bylaws would put the legal paperwork in place just in case.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — if approved, this could affect the City's future borrowing capacity and property tax levels.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council meeting — loan guarantee bylaws vote

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Vote on Bike Learning Zone Location and Grant Funding
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on approving 7679 18th Street (in the Edmonds area) as the site for Burnaby's first bike learning zone — a car-free space where kids can learn to ride and practice road safety. Council will also consider directing staff to apply for outside grant funding to help pay for the design and construction of the facility, which is estimated to cost around $350,000 to build.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — grant applications and construction timelines depend on this vote.

key_dates.label: ICBC Community Grants Program application deadline — Bike Learning Zone

briefs.title: Council to vote on converting 35 affordable rental units to market-rate at 5227 Lane Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a developer's request to convert 35 below-market rental units (priced at CMHC median rates — a federally set affordability benchmark) into regular market-rate rentals at a new 70-unit building at 5227 Lane Street. The developer says rising construction costs and financing charges have made the project unviable without this change, and Council will consider repealing the existing housing agreement that locked in the affordable units.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision — it removes 35 below-market rental units from this Burnaby development.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Council vote — 5227 Lane Street affordable rental conversion public meeting

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Finance & Insurance

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to Vote on 2026–2030 Financial Plan and Budget Bylaw
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on Burnaby's 2026–2030 Financial Plan and its accompanying Financial Plan Bylaw — a legally required five-year spending and revenue blueprint. If approved, the plan would introduce a 2.9% property tax increase, hold water rates flat at 0.0%, raise sewer rates by 3.5%, and maintain a 1.9% Growth Infrastructure Investment Levy, while authorizing $506.8 million in capital spending for 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — tax and utility rate changes will appear on your 2026 property tax and utility bills.

key_dates.label: Burnaby Financial Plan Bylaw 2026 — expected adoption date
Burnaby Tax Rates Bylaw 2026 — adoption deadline
Burnaby 2026 property tax bills mailed to residents

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to Consider New Non-Residential Zoning Districts Under Burnaby 2050 Plan
briefs.summary: At its January 13, 2026 meeting, Burnaby City Council reviewed several proposals with potential impacts on local businesses. Most notably, a proposed zoning bylaw rewrite would create five new non-residential zoning districts as part of the Burnaby 2050 land-use plan — but Council sent it back to staff for more information after raising concerns about setbacks, unclear use definitions, and restrictions on recycling facilities.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the revised zoning bylaw report — it could affect where and how your business can operate in Burnaby.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
